description: Create a content slide on business considerations for AI productivity with a full-width strategy matrix table comparing early adopters, followers, and late adopters across operating model, competitive positioning, financials, and technology/compliance.

header.headline: Businesses must adapt operating models and capabilities as AI maturity drives widening performance gaps
header.sub: Strategic considerations across different levels of AI adoption

rows:
Organizations already embedding AI across core workflows
Strong capabilities, use cases, and growing returns

• Redesign for end-to-end workflow
• Enable greater agility and adaptability within people and process to disruption

• Differentiating AI-enabled services across the streams e.g., Propositions, Pricing
• Realise higher productivity as AI maturity advances
• New revenue models enabled by AI (e.g., personalised services) prioritisation

• Emphasis on governance as models grow more complex
• Best positioned to leverage agentic AI within existing process

Organizations with meaningful AI opportunities but uneven progress
Selective adoption and barriers e.g., regulatory, capability or data restraints

• Prioritise the highest-value use cases to avoid fragmented adoption
• Seamless shift towards standardised decision workflows
• Scale priority initiatives while driving targeted innovation ahead of competitors

• Investments with areas of visible ROI to avoid experimental sprawl
• Is there enough financial evidence to support scaling decisions?
• Address data quality, access and barriers
Organizations where AI adoption remains limited due to operational constraints, legacy systems or low applicability

• Focus on foundational digital and process improvements
– Specific areas (e.g., reporting)
– Improving data literacy
• Risk of losing market share if further behind competitors
